2605.23218.title: Foundation Protocol: A Coordination Layer for Agentic Society
2605.23218.paper_summary: Autonomous agents are moving from tools into a layer of social infrastructure: they browse, purchase, deploy software, manage systems, and increasingly interact with one another. As these systems scale, the bottleneck shifts away from raw model capability toward coordination. Agents need to form reliable relationships, organize multi-agent work, exchange value, support an AI economy, and stay safe and accountable under real-world oversight. This paper introduces the Foundation Protocol (FP), a graph-first coordination layer for an emerging human-AI society. FP unifies heterogeneous entities, including agents, tools, resources, humans, institutions, and organizations, and supports native multi-party organization and event-based collaboration. It also provides economic primitives for metering, receipts, and settlement, and treats policy, provenance, and audit as first-class concerns. FP is designed to wrap and bridge existing protocols rather than replace them, enabling incremental adoption while reducing integration and governance overhead. The aim is to keep autonomous agency composable while keeping accountability non-negotiable, so that coordination itself can become shared infrastructure for a human-AI society that is open, pluralistic, and governable.
2605.23218.paper_summary_zh: 自主代理正在從工具轉變為社會基礎設施的一部分：它們瀏覽、購買、部署軟體、管理系統，並且越來越多地相互互動。隨著這些系統的擴展，瓶頸從原始模型能力轉向協調。代理需要建立可靠的關係，組織多代理工作，交換價值，支持人工智慧經濟，並在現實世界的監督下保持安全和負責任。本文介紹了基礎協議（Foundation Protocol, FP），這是一個以圖為主的協調層，旨在為新興的人類與人工智慧社會提供支持。FP統一了異質實體，包括代理、工具、資源、人類、機構和組織，並支持原生的多方組織和基於事件的協作。它還提供計量、收據和結算的經濟原語，並將政策、來源和審計視為首要關注事項。FP旨在包裝和橋接現有協議，而非取而代之，從而實現漸進式採用，同時減少整合和治理的負擔。其目標是保持自主代理的可組合性，同時使問責制不可協商，從而使協調本身能夠成為一個開放、多元且可治理的人類與人工智慧社會的共享基礎設施。
2605.23218.author: Bang Liu et.al.
2605.23218.authors: Bang Liu, Yongfeng Gu, Jiayi Zhang, Zhaoyang Yu, Sirui Hong, Maojia Song, Xiaoqiang Wang, Mingyi Deng, Zijie Zhuang, Ronghao Wang, Mingzhe Cao, Yutong Zhu, Xingjian Li, Yifan Wu, Jianhao Ruan, Yiran Peng, Shuangrui Chen, Jinlin Wang, Yizhang Lin, Dongjie Zhang, Dekun Wu, Chen Ma, Lizi Liao, Han Yu, Jian Pei, Heng Ji, Qiang Yang, Yuyu Luo, Chenglin Wu
